delegationRole: The Chinese delegation, publicly neutral on Kashmiri governance while working to preserve the territorial status quo, protect its claims and its corridor to the Arabian Sea, and block any precedent of internationally sanctioned separatism.
fundamentalPrinciples: Chinese national sovereignty encompasses Aksai Chin, Arunachal Pradesh, and the Shaksam Valley in Gilgit-Baltistan.
Any resolution must preserve access to economic opportunities via the Arabian Sea.
Chinese regional influence demonstrates its international prestige.

desiredEndStates.primary: Alleviation of India–Pakistan tensions, increased regional stability, and preservation of the status quo in Jammu and Kashmir with the Line of Control continuing to divide Indian and Pakistani administration.
desiredEndStates.alternate: If no agreement, the status quo persists unchanged — an outcome we can accept — provided no autonomy precedent and no international investigation are established.

keyPositionsByIssue.TERRITORY: Chinese sovereignty over Aksai Chin, Arunachal Pradesh and the Shaksam Valley is historical and legitimate and will not be conceded. The McMahon Line was arbitrarily drawn by the British Empire and carries no legal legitimacy.
keyPositionsByIssue.LOC-STATUS: We hope to uphold the Line of Control between Pakistan and India; supporting it as the boundary keeps the territorial conflict bilateral between them.
keyPositionsByIssue.GOVERNANCE: Publicly neutral. In substance, an autonomous or independent Jammu and Kashmir as a legitimized entity separate from both Pakistan and India would upend the regional balance and threaten our territorial claims.
keyPositionsByIssue.PLEBISCITE: Limit the possibility of any plebiscite on independence, and oppose any ballot including autonomy or independence.
keyPositionsByIssue.KASHMIRI-DELEGATION: We will speak to every party except the Jammu & Kashmir delegation bilaterally; recognizing separatist movements might inspire similar movements within our own territories. We may participate in multilateral sessions where they are present.
keyPositionsByIssue.INVESTIGATIONS: Human rights are fundamentally a domestic issue. Oppose international investigations to avoid setting a dangerous precedent in our sphere of influence; India and Pakistan should resolve their accusations by internal review.
keyPositionsByIssue.IDP-REFUGEES: As a confidence-building measure we will provide monetary relief aid for the roughly 950,000 displaced; the figure can be negotiated later so long as the concept is accepted. Aid must not be misappropriated by insurgent groups.
keyPositionsByIssue.NUCLEAR: Relative neutrality, so long as both programs remain genuine deterrents. They restrained India in the 1986-87 Brasstacks crisis, the 1990 compound crisis and after the 2008 Mumbai attacks. We have supported and will continue to support Pakistan's deterrent.
keyPositionsByIssue.CPEC-ACCESS: Pakistan's continued control of Azad Kashmir supports our access to the Arabian Sea via Gwadar; we will forward no new claims on the Shaksam Valley that would sour Islamabad.

redLines: No consolidated Indian or Pakistani claim on the entirety of Jammu and Kashmir.
No agreement in which China forfeits territorial claims.
No change in the security status of Chinese forces in Aksai Chin.
No plebiscite allowing a vote on an autonomous or independent Jammu and Kashmir.
No international human rights investigations in the region.
batna: Do nothing and win: the status quo already secures our claims, our corridor and our position, so we can decline any package without cost and continue bilaterally.
concessionsWilling: Provide a substantial financial contribution to relief for the displaced as a confidence-building measure.
Support the Line of Control as the continuing line of administration between India and Pakistan.
Maintain public neutrality on Kashmiri governance rather than pressing our own formula.
Work toward stability and de-escalation on the Sino-Indian frontier.
Cooperate with Russia and Pakistan on screening displaced populations against militant infiltration.
Forward no additional claims on the Shaksam Valley currently administered by Islamabad.
negotiatingStyle: Measured major-country diplomacy with an edge held in reserve: prefers bilateral rooms, concedes money rather than principle, and blocks precedents rather than arguing outcomes.
privateInstructions: Balance Zhànláng Wàijiāo with a measured approach; take an active role under President Xi's dàguó-wàijiāo, and remember the times and trends are on our side.
Prefer bilateral formats and minimize multilateral discussion, which dilutes our strength and drifts away from our concerns.
You may not meet the Jammu and Kashmir delegation bilaterally; multilateral sessions including them are permitted.
Seek common ground with Russia to counterbalance and marginalize the position of the United States.
A demilitarized Jammu and Kashmir would free Indian forces for the Sino-Indian border — preserving the status quo avoids that and avoids confrontation with India.
Any concession on Aksai Chin would set a dangerous precedent that Tibetan separatists could exploit; the all-weather highway between Tibet and Xinjiang runs through it.
Weaken support for the Kashmiri delegation's objectives from within any of the participating delegations.
A substantial financial offer will assure Pakistan of our friendship and increase its support on territorial issues.
Route periodic reports and requests for guidance to the office of the Foreign Minister.
